data.title: Great Elm Capital Corp. prices $50 million public offering of 7.75% Notes due 2030, net proceeds approx $48.1 million.
data.description: Palm Beach Gardens, Florida, Sept. 04, 2025 - Great Elm Capital Corp. (GECC) disclosed the pricing details of its underwritten public offering, amounting to $50.0 million in aggregate principal notes with a 7.75% interest rate maturing in 2030. The Company is estimated to receive net proceeds around $48.1 million post the deduction of underwriting discounts, commissions, and expected offering expenses.

data.title: Politicians respond to BBC Documentary - Football's Financial Shame - The Story of the V11, uncovering financial abuse scandal among top footballers.
data.description: In a recent parliamentary statement in London on September 4, 2025, officials addressed the BBC documentary "Football's Financial Shame – The Story of the V11," which uncovered a concerning instance of financial exploitation within a group of esteemed footballers. The documentary highlighted the inappropriate handling of funds among these players, raising significant alarm. The exposed financial misconduct has stirred discussions and calls for accountability among the public and football authorities. The incident underscores the need for increased transparency and oversight in the management of finances within the sports industry.
Transcontinental Inc. reveals Q3 FY2025 figures, reports revenue of $500 million and net income of $50 million.

data.title: Hims & Hers' personalized health products led to a 1,600% share price increase in less than 4 years, with a 90+ Relative Strength Rating.
data.description: Hims & Hers Health has achieved a remarkable Relative Strength Rating surpassing 90, indicating exceptional performance. The company's focused approach on tailored health products resulted in a staggering 1,600% surge in its share price within a span of less than four years. This growth exemplifies the strong market response to Hims & Hers offerings.

data.title: CarMax (NYSE: KMX) to report Q2 financial results on Sept 25, 2025, with a conference call at 9:00 a.m. ET. Key participants will include Bill Nash, CEO, Enrique Mayor-Mora, CFO, and Jon Daniels, EVP.
data.description: CarMax, Inc. (NYSE:KMX) based in Richmond, Virginia, is set to release its financial results for the second quarter ending on August 31, 2025, prior to the market opening on September 25, 2025. A conference call with investors is scheduled for 9:00 a.m. ET on the same day to review these results. Key speakers on the call will be Bill Nash, the President and CEO of CarMax, Enrique Mayor-Mora, the Executive Vice President and CFO, and Jon Daniels, the Executive Vice President of CarMax Auto Finance. Investors and interested parties can access the live conference call.

data.title: Allegion plc declares quarterly dividend of $0.51 per share, payable on Sept. 30, 2025, with shareholders of record on Sept. 15, 2025.
data.description: Allegion plc (NYSE: ALLE), a prominent provider of security products and solutions globally, recently disclosed a quarterly dividend of $0.51 per ordinary share. This dividend will be paid out on Sept. 30, 2025, to shareholders listed on the records by Sept. 15, 2025. Allegion focuses on producing cutting-edge security and access solutions aimed at ensuring safety in various environments like residential, educational, commercial, and connected spaces.

data.title: Eric Jackson holds bullish view on Opendoor Technologies, projecting targets as high as $82 per share. OPEN stock has gained 140% in the past month. He also holds iShares Ethereum Trust ETF (ETHA).
data.description: Eric Jackson, the founder of EMJ Capital, has expressed his strong optimism regarding Opendoor Technologies, Inc. (NASDAQ: OPEN), marking it as a potential "100-bagger" with a forecasted target price of up to $82 per share. The stock for OPEN has observed a significant 140% increase over the course of the past month. Jackson also holds a selection of other high-conviction stocks that he has labeled as "longs" on his social media platform. One such stock is the iShares Ethereum Trust ETF (NASDAQ: ETHA), an exchange-traded fund managed by BlackRock that follows the price movements of Ethereum (CRYPTO: ETH). Jackson views this investment as a strategic move to capitalize on the growing institutional adoption of cryptocurrency assets. For further insights, the complete article can be accessed on Benzinga.com.

data.title: Dell Technologies increases quarterly dividend to $0.525 per share, raising annual dividend by 18% to $2.10 per share.
data.description: Dell Technologies, headquartered in Round Rock, Texas, recently announced a quarterly cash dividend of $0.525 per common share for its shareholders. This dividend will be paid on Oct. 31, with shareholders of record as of Oct. 21 being eligible. The company also approved an 18% increase in its annual cash dividend to $2.10 per common share earlier this year. Dell Technologies, listed as NYSE: DELL, focuses on assisting organizations and individuals in navigating the digital landscape and undergoing transformation.

data.title: Tecsys Q1 2026: SaaS revenue up by 25% to $19.1M, Total revenue at $46M, Adjusted EBITDA at $3.2M, Acquired 21,300 shares.
data.description: Tecsys Inc., a supply chain management SaaS company, reported a 25% increase in SaaS revenue and a 24% rise in adjusted EBITDA for the first quarter of fiscal 2026, ended July 31, 2025. SaaS revenue reached $19.1 million, SaaS ARR increased to $79.3 million, and SaaS RPO grew to $226.3 million. Total revenue rose to $46.0 million, with net profit at $0.8 million and adjusted EBITDA at $3.2 million. Tecsys maintained its full fiscal 2026 guidance, including total revenue growth of 8-10%, SaaS revenue growth of 20-22%, and adjusted EBITDA margin of 8-9%. The company also announced a quarterly dividend of $0.085 per share. Tecsys' financial results conference call is set for September 5, 2025. Non-IFRS performance measures such as EBITDA and adjusted EBITDA were highlighted to provide insights into their financial performance. Furthermore, their Condensed Interim Consolidated Statements of Financial Position (Unaudited) were provided, showcasing their current assets totaling $78,522 as of July 31, 2025. Visit www.tecsys.com for more information about Tecsys and its solutions.

data.title: Taco Bell Canada launches Encore Hours in Vancouver for late-night snacking during Frosh Week, catering to students and night owls.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, Taco Bell Canada is introducing Encore Hours, a unique late-night snacking opportunity for students and night owls during frosh week. As many eateries close by midnight, residents often struggle to find late-night dining options. However, with Encore Hours, they can enjoy both fun and food without compromises. Having previously launched successfully in the UK in August 2024, this initiative aims to cater to the needs of the community. This new offering will allow customers to satisfy their late-night cravings and enjoy delicious snacks even after regular restaurant hours, enhancing the dining experience.

data.title: 'CopyPasta' attack exploits AI with fake licenses, infecting at scale. Security firm HiddenLayer raises alarm.
data.description: Security company HiddenLayer has issued a warning that AI coding tools are susceptible to being deceived by counterfeit license files, leading to the dissemination of harmful code. The firm emphasizes the potential risks associated with this vulnerability, highlighting the need for heightened vigilance in the use of AI technology. By exploiting this weakness, malicious actors could inject harmful code into software applications, posing a significant threat to cybersecurity. This alert underscores the importance of robust security measures to prevent the inadvertent distribution of malicious software.

data.title: Deborah Gracio to lead Pacific Northwest National Laboratory, starting on October 1. A 35-year veteran at PNNL, she was selected after a competitive national search.
data.description: In Columbus, Ohio, Battelle revealed today the appointment of Deborah (Deb) Gracio as the upcoming director of Pacific Northwest National Laboratory (PNNL), starting October 1. Gracio will take over from Steven Ashby, who earlier this year mentioned his plan to resign as director. Battelle manages PNNL on behalf of the U.S. Department of Energy (DOE). With 35 years of experience at PNNL, Gracio, chosen through a rigorous national search, presently holds the position of associate laboratory director.

data.title: Ametros names Shawn Deane General Counsel, overseeing legal, regulatory ops. Deane has nearly 20 years of experience.
data.description: Wilmington, Massachusetts - Ametros, a division of Webster Bank, N.A. specializing in professional administration of medical insurance claims settlements, welcomes Shawn Deane back as General Counsel. Deane, who is also Managing Director at Webster Bank, will now lead Ametros' legal, regulatory, and compliance divisions as a key member of its executive team. With almost 20 years of experience, Deane brings valuable insight from his prior role at Ametros to further enhance the company's operations.

data.title: Rayonier Inc. releases 2024 Sustainability Report showcasing actions to enhance sustainability, meet society's needs. CEO Mark McHugh shares vision.
data.description: In WILDLIGHT, Florida, Rayonier Inc. (NYSE: RYN) has unveiled its 2024 Sustainability Report, showcasing efforts to improve sustainability on its land holdings and ensure the well-being of investors, employees, communities, and stakeholders. Mark McHugh, President and CEO, emphasized the company's goal of maximizing land resource potential to serve societal needs. The report reflects Rayonier's commitment to long-term sustainability and outlines strategies to achieve it. This news signifies Rayonier's dedication to integrating sustainability practices across its operations.

data.title: Kraken's Breakout acquisition propels trading infrastructure expansion post $1.5B NinjaTrader buyout in May 2025.
data.description: Kraken is expanding its trading infrastructure efforts after acquiring NinjaTrader for $1.5 billion in May 2025.

data.title: Global DNA Manufacturing Market to reach USD 19.56 Bn by 2032 with 17.2% CAGR. Growing demand for gene therapies and advancements in synthetic biology fueling market growth. High production costs and technological advancements influencing growth prospects.
data.description: The Global DNA Manufacturing Market is projected to be worth USD 6.44 billion in 2025 and is anticipated to reach USD 19.56 billion by 2032, with a CAGR of 17.2% during this period. Factors driving this growth include increasing demand for gene therapies, vaccines, and personalized medicine, as well as advancements in synthetic biology and CRISPR-based technologies. The market is fueled by rising investments in genomics research, expanding applications in drug discovery, and the prevalence of genetic disorders. Investments from governments, partnerships in the biopharma sector, and the need for scalable and cost-efficient DNA synthesis solutions are also boosting market expansion. The synthetic DNA segment is expected to hold nearly half of the market share by 2025, with oncology being the largest application, capturing 24.3% of revenue. North America presently dominates with a market share of 38.5%, while Asia Pacific is foreseen as a lucrative region in the future. Despite growth, high production costs remain a challenge due to the complexity and expense of manufacturing high-quality DNA for clinical use. Nonetheless, technological advancements in genetic engineering and synthetic biology, led by companies like GenScript and Twist Bioscience, are creating new growth opportunities by accelerating the synthesis of complex DNA sequences and opening up fresh revenue streams for the DNA manufacturing market.
